title: Tea Market Studies: Egypt, Islamic Republic of Iran, Pakistan and Turkey (FAO Intergovernmental Group on Tea, 16th Session, Bali 2005)
source_org: FAO Committee on Commodity Problems, Intergovernmental Group on Tea
units: thousand tonnes (volume); kg per capita per year (Table 6); document report CCP:TE 05/CRS 2
license_terms: FAO document, publicly disclosed committee paper
notes: Downloaded directly via curl (no block, unlike many iranicaonline.org/Iran-domestic-site fetches this project has hit). Table 5 (Iran tea imports/exports/consumption 1994-2003) and Table 6 (Iran tea consumption per capita 1998-2003, with population denominator) transcribed verbatim from the FAO document HTML (cross-checked: WebFetch summary matched a direct curl+regex extraction of the same page, both independently arrived at identical figures). Iran per-capita tea consumption averaged 1.23 kg/year over 1998-2003 -- corroborates Iran being among the highest per-capita tea-consuming nations historically, though notably LOWER than Turkey (2.11 kg in 2003) despite common popular framing of Iran as the higher consumer; smuggled/unrecorded imports (est. up to 30% of legal imports per source para 29) mean true consumption may be understated. Turkey/Egypt/Pakistan figures included only where the same source table already covered them (no extra fetch cost) per project Iran-first comparator policy. Complements (does not duplicate) the existing FAOSTAT-derived tea per-capita figures noted in web research (FAOSTAT shows a implausible-looking 2.94 kg 2013 all-time-high vs 0.26 kg 2007 all-time-low per a Helgi Library summary — NOT independently verified or saved to this database; flagged as a discrepancy worth a future cross-check against this more granular, methodologically-transparent FAO study).
